title: RabbitMQ 4.3 Retries, Priorities, and Consumer Timeouts · Go Semantics Case: Quorum Priority Levels
summary: A Go 1.26 service team must model 4.3 delayed retries, strict priorities, consumer timeouts, delivery counts, and feature-state rollout with client evidence; reviewers reject hidden goroutine ownership, unbounded work, stale channel state, and claims that stop at a nil client error. The learner predicts values, ownership transitions, and stop paths before seeing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the quorum priority levels increment in a Go client and broker-policy compatibility record.…
objectives: Use RabbitMQ 4.3 quorum 32 strict priority levels with bounded use, starvation evidence, prefetch interaction, and per-priority monitoring; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Compare native delayed retries with TTL and DLX cycles by rewrite cost, loss and duplicate windows, head-of-line effects, topology, and version support; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Predict Quorum Priority Levels · Theory Quorum Priority Levels
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go messaging team handles case 2788 during race-enabled integration test with duplicate delivery. Build deterministic Go evidence to enforce RabbitMQ 4.3 delayed retry, strict priority, and consumer-timeout policy limits; prove cleanup under a single deadline and cancellation cause; then remove the parking binding and expose the remediation gap. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use RabbitMQ 4.3 quorum 32 strict priority levels with bounded use, starvation evidence, prefetch interaction, and per-priority monitoring; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
